Lawn fertilizer service in Guelph

Slow-release granular fertilizer, applied at the right time for Ontario lawns — with plain-English watering instructions after every visit.

★★★★★  75+ five-star Google reviews

The service

Thicker, greener grass — without the program trap

Most fertilizer companies in Guelph sell annual programs with auto-renewal and prepay enrollment. We don’t. Order a single application, a few key ones, or a full season — whatever your lawn actually needs.

  • Slow-release granular fertilizer suited to Ontario turf
  • Applications timed to the season, not a sales calendar
  • Written watering instructions after every application
  • Overseeding available to thicken thin or patchy areas
  • Pairs perfectly with weekly mowing — one crew, one invoice
Get a fertilizer quote
After-care

What to do after we fertilize

Every application comes with simple after-care instructions, because fertilizer only works if it gets watered in properly. The short version we give every client:

  • Water within 24 hours — a good soak moves nutrients to the roots
  • Then water deeply once a week — about an inch, rather than daily sprinkles
  • Keep pets off until it’s watered in and the lawn has dried
  • Keep mowing at 3 inches — taller grass makes better use of the feed

Guelph’s Outside Water Use Bylaw can restrict watering in dry stretches — when a level is in effect, we’ll time applications around it so nothing goes to waste. For the full seasonal timing, read our guide on when to fertilize your lawn in Ontario.

Local know-how

When to fertilize in Guelph — and what most people get wrong

Guelph lawns are cool-season turf — mostly Kentucky bluegrass and fescue — and they feed on a different clock than the bags at the store suggest. The two feedings that matter most are late spring, once the lawn is fully awake and past its first flush of growth, and early fall, when roots are rebuilding for winter. A September feeding does more for next summer's lawn than anything you can do in July.

The most common mistake we see is fertilizing in the heat of mid-summer. Pushing growth during a drought stresses the lawn, burns easily, and mostly feeds weeds. The second is skipping the watering-in: granular fertilizer sitting dry on the blade does nothing. That's why every Yardex application comes with written watering instructions for exactly your week's forecast.

Expect visible results in ten to fourteen days: deeper colour first, then thicker growth. Pair fall fertilizer with overseeding and you fix thin patches at the same time the lawn is feeding — one visit, two jobs done.

Common questions

Frequently asked

How many fertilizer applications does a Guelph lawn need?

Most healthy lawns do well with two to three well-timed applications: late spring, early fall, and optionally late fall. More isn't better — timing beats volume. We'll tell you honestly if your lawn only needs one.

Is your fertilizer safe for kids and dogs?

We use granular slow-release fertilizer, not liquid pesticide sprays. Once it's watered in — typically after the first watering or rainfall — the lawn is fine for normal family use. We leave instructions after every visit.

Do I have to sign up for a season program?

No. That's the main difference between Yardex and the big fertilizer franchises: order one application or a full season, no auto-renewal, no prepay pressure.

When should I water after an application?

Within 24–48 hours, about 20–30 minutes per zone, unless rain is coming. Exact instructions come with every visit because the right answer depends on that week's forecast.

Can you fix bare or thin patches too?

Yes — we pair fertilizer with overseeding in spring or early fall. Seed plus feed in one visit is the most cost-effective way to thicken a patchy lawn.

Why does my lawn look worse in August even with fertilizer?

That's summer dormancy, not a fertilizer problem. Cool-season grass naturally slows in heat. Fed lawns bounce back faster in September — that's the payoff.
